title: 16S Amplicon PCR for the V3-V4 region for MicroCOPD samples
author: Tuyen Hoang
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.2sygefw

steps.title: Thaw DNA samples
steps.instruction: Select and thaw DNA samples for PCR.

steps.title: Plan PCR layout
steps.instruction: Create an overview excel sheet and a plate- and pipetting-layout for the PCR run.

steps.title: Spin DNA samples
steps.instruction: Spin DNA samples at 13000 x g for 1 minute at room temperature.

steps.title: Transfer DNA to PCR plate
steps.instruction: Pipette 5 μl of each DNA sample into a well on a PCR plate, following the plate- and pipetting-layout.

steps.title: Prepare PCR master mix
steps.instruction: Prepare a master mix containing the following components, in 2 Eppendorf tubes in a prePCR-hood/room. Vortex briefly to mix, and spin briefly to bring down the content.

steps.title: Add master mix to PCR plate
steps.instruction: Combine the master mix from the two Eppendorf-tubes in a sterile trough. Using a multichannel pipette, transfer 20 μl to each well of the PCR plate. Pipette up and down 10 times to mix.

steps.title: Seal PCR plate
steps.instruction: Seal the PCR plate with MicroAmp film.

steps.title: Centrifuge PCR plate
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the PCR plate at 1000 x g for 1 minute at 20 °C.

steps.title: Run PCR
steps.instruction: Run PCR in a thermal cycler using the following program.

steps.title: Store or proceed with gel electrophoresis
steps.instruction: Continue with Agarose Gel Electrophoresis for checking of 16S PCR amplicons or store PCR plate at -20°C until use.

steps.title: Thaw and spin amplicons
steps.instruction: Thaw and spin down 16S amplicons in Amplicon PCR plate at 1000 x g at 20°C for 1 minute.

steps.title: Cast agarose gels
steps.instruction: Cast two big 1.5% agarose gels containing GelRed (per gel use: 3g agarose + 200 ml 1xTAE + 10 μl GelRed). Use four 15-lane combs per gel.

steps.title: Add loading buffer
steps.instruction: On a new 96-well plate, add 1 μl 5x DNA loading buffer (use a multichannel pipette and pipette from buffer aliquots in 8 PCR tubes).

steps.title: Transfer amplicons to loading plate
steps.instruction: Using a multichannel pipette, transfer 5 μl of 16S amplicons from the Amplicon PCR plate to the 96-well plate containing 5x DNA loading buffer. Pipette up and down gently to mix.

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge loading plate
steps.instruction: Centrifuge plate briefly (10 seconds at 1000 x g) to bring down the content.

steps.title: Load DNA ladder
steps.instruction: Load EZ 100bp ladder (3 µl) on the first lane of each lane-row.

steps.title: Load samples
steps.instruction: Load samples using a multichannel pipette (remember to note down the sample order in loading).

steps.title: Run gel (initial)
steps.instruction: Run gel in 1xTAE buffer at 80V for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Run gel (extended)
steps.instruction: Increase to 100V and run until the bromophenol blue marker has moved ca. 2/3 of the gel (i.e. 1 hour). If 1% is used, shorten running time!

steps.title: Prepare AMPure XP beads
steps.instruction: Bring the AMPure XP beads to room temperature.
